verifier_recommendations_addressed: Provide unprotected workbook view or proof-of-formulas sheet

planned_changes: Add a new worksheet named 'ProofOfFormulas' that mirrors all critical calculation cells and uses FORMULATEXT (or visible formula strings) so auditors can read exact formulas. For environments without FORMULATEXT, add a table of cell references and the exact formula strings as plain text.

Unprotect the workbook or produce an unprotected copy; if protection is required, also provide an exported PDF or CSV snapshot of the key sheets (Inputs, Scenarios, Recommendation) showing formulas and values.

Complete the expert_memo.md Recommendation table and add an appendix mapping each recommendation row to exact workbook cell ranges and formula expressions used to compute the scenario results and budget feasibility checks.

Correct grader/verifier-related metadata fields: remove contradictory statements and add a short 'semantic_evidence' section that lists the memo lines, actual_outputs.json entries, and workbook cell ranges that justify any semantic_score assigned.

Attach an exported proof artifact (e.g., proof_of_formulas.csv or proof_of_formulas.pdf) alongside safety_PLC_3.xlsx in the artifacts directory so verifiers can open a text-based representation even if spreadsheet libraries are missing.
Regenerate actual_outputs.json (if needed) to include a short checksum or line-by-line mapping to workbook cells used for each scenario result, to make the numeric provenance explicit.

Re-run the verification/grading step and include the updated grader/verifier metadata in the final submission so reviewers can confirm that formula-level auditability and metadata consistency issues are resolved.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Provide formula-level auditable evidence and resolve metadata inconsistencies so verifier can confirm semantic correctness without parsing the .xlsx binary in the runtime environment.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables present and consistent with the provided memo and normalized actual_outputs. Deterministic artifact-contract checks passed. Two issues require remediation before final audit acceptance: 1) The grader/verifier could not parse the .xlsx binary in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so in-sheet formulas were not directly inspected. 2) Metadata contains inconsistent statements about whether semantic correctness was evaluated (some entries state semantic evaluation was not performed while others report semantic_score/semantic_correctness_score=1.0). The memo preview is helpful but truncated and the Recommendation table was not fully visible in the preview.
